question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with both signedTransactionBytes and bodyBytes populated so that parseTransaction() selects signedTransactionBytes first, causing the mirror node to parse a different TransactionBody than the one visible in the legacy bodyBytes field, silently suppressing the gossip record of the bodyBytes transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction proto where signedTransactionBytes is non-empty but contains a SignedTransaction with an empty bodyBytes, causing TransactionBody.parseFrom() to return an empty TransactionBody, so that the resulting RecordItem has a null/empty transactionBody and the transaction is never gossiped or persisted?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose signedTransactionBytes contains a valid SignedTransaction but with a deliberately malformed bodyBytes that triggers InvalidProtocolBufferException, causing a ProtobufException to be thrown and the entire transaction (and potentially the whole record file batch) to be dropped from gossip processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction proto where all three paths (signedTransactionBytes, bodyBytes, hasBody) are empty or absent, causing parseTransaction() to throw ProtobufException(BAD_TRANSACTION_BODY_BYTES_MESSAGE) and preventing the transaction from being gossiped or recorded?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the deprecated transaction.getBody() fallback path in parseTransaction() to inject a TransactionBody that differs from the one in signedTransactionBytes, causing the mirror node to gossip an incorrect or attacker-controlled transaction type while the real transaction is suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isTopLevel()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with transactionNonce=0 and a parentConsensusTimestamp set in the TransactionRecord, causing isTopLevel() to return true (due to nonce==0 short-circuit) even though the transaction is actually a child, thereby bypassing child-transaction gossip suppression logic and causing the transaction to be processed as top-level?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isTopLevel()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with transactionNonce > 0 and scheduled=true, causing isTopLevel() to return true via the scheduled branch, thereby preventing the mirror node from correctly identifying and suppressing gossip of internal/child transactions that should not be top-level?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isTopLevel()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction record that lacks a parentConsensusTimestamp (hasParentConsensusTimestamp() returns false) even though the transaction is a child, causing isTopLevel() to return true and the child transaction to be gossiped as a top-level transaction, violating the parent-child gossip ordering invariant?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isTopLevel()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with nonce=0 and scheduled=false and no parentConsensusTimestamp, causing isTopLevel() to return true for every such transaction regardless of its actual role in a batch, flooding the gossip layer with transactions that should be suppressed as internal?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isSystemFileUpdate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FileUpdate transaction body and set the payerAccountId in the TransactionBody's TransactionID to account number 50 (matching the system account check in isSystemFileUpdate()), causing isTopLevel() to return true via the isSystemFileUpdate() branch and allowing the transaction to bypass child-transaction gossip suppression?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isSystemFileUpdate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isSystemFileUpdate() only checks transactionBody.hasFileUpdate() and payerAccountId==0.0.50 without verifying the actual file being updated, causing any FileUpdate transaction paid by account 50 to be treated as top-level and gossiped even when it should be suppressed as a child transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isSystemFileUpdate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the TransactionBody contains a FileUpdate but the payerAccountId in the TransactionID is set to shard/realm/50 in a non-default shard or realm, causing isSystemFileUpdate() to fail the EntityId.equals() check and the transaction to be incorrectly classified as non-top-level, suppressing its gossip?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a child transaction whose parentConsensusTimestamp matches the consensusTimestamp of an unrelated previous transaction in the stream, causing parseParent() to incorrectly assign a wrong parent and potentially suppress or misroute the gossip of the child transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with a parentConsensusTimestamp that matches previous.parent.transactionRecord.getParentConsensusTimestamp() (the grandparent path in parseParent()), causing the transaction to be assigned a grandparent as its parent and silently suppressing the gossip of the actual parent-child relationship?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a batch of transactions where the parentConsensusTimestamp of a child does not match any of the three lookup paths in parseParent() (previous, previous.parent, previous.parent.parent), causing the child to fall back to this.parent (which may be null), effectively orphaning the child and preventing it from being gossiped with correct parent context?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction stream where two consecutive transactions share the same consensusTimestamp, causing parseParent() timestamp equality checks to match the wrong transaction as parent and silently suppressing the gossip of the legitimate parent-child pair?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCall transaction to contract number 365 (HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM) with a parentConsensusTimestamp, causing parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to skip the hook parent search (due to the contractNum != HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM guard), and the transaction to be processed without a hookParent, suppressing hook-related gossip?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction record with a ContractCallResult whose contractID.contractNum is exactly 365 (HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM), causing parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to return that transaction as the hookParent for a sibling, thereby injecting a false hook parent and corrupting the gossip chain for multiple transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a long chain of previous RecordItems all with ContractResults but none with contractNum==365, causing the while loop in parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to traverse the entire previous chain up to parent without finding a hook, resulting in null hookParent and suppressing hook-triggered gossip for the current transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the current transaction's ContractResult has contractNum==365 (HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM), causing the condition currentTxnRecordContractResult.getContractID().getContractNum() != HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM to be false and the entire hook parent search to be skipped, preventing the transaction from being linked to its hook parent and suppressing its gossip?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a child transaction whose parent RecordItem has isSuccessful()==false, causing parseSuccess() to return false for the child regardless of the child's own receipt status, and thereby suppressing the gossip of the child transaction as if it failed even when the network recorded it as SUCCESS?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction record with receipt status FEE_SCHEDULE_FILE_PART_UPLOADED, causing parseSuccess() to return true and the transaction to be gossiped as successful, even though this status is only valid for system-level fee schedule uploads, potentially allowing a non-system transaction to bypass gossip suppression?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction record with receipt status SUCCESS_BUT_MISSING_EXPECTED_OPERATION, causing parseSuccess() to return true and the transaction to be gossiped as fully successful, potentially bypassing downstream gossip filters that check for partial success conditions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the parent success inheritance in parseSuccess() by injecting a failed parent RecordItem into the previous chain, causing all child transactions in a batch to be marked as unsuccessful and suppressed from gossip, effectively preventing multiple legitimate transactions from being recorded?
